key_points: Quadratic equations can be solved using factorisation or quadratic formula
Discriminant determines the nature of roots
Quadratic equations can have two, one or no real roots
Graphs of quadratic equations are parabolas
Roots of a quadratic equation are x-intercepts of its graph
exam_tips: If factorisation is difficult, use formula
Always simplify equation to standard form
Relate roots with graph whenever possible
